spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the feature's general idea but doesn't surface the named spec API symbols directly.

interactive.rationale: Real controls + script-driven side-effects make this hands-on, not a static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Most of the blurb's promises land but a few of the named behaviours aren't shown.

edge_cases.rationale: Page handles several edge cases (errors, fallbacks, feature detection, denials) — not just the happy path.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> reference link as required.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex, button overrides keep bg+color paired.

openQuestions.title: Surface more named spec API for prerendering-cross-origin-iframes
openQuestions.detail: The spec summary lists API surfaces this concept doesn't currently name on-page. Either rename the controls / readouts to mention them, or add a second concept page that targets the missing surface directly. The reviewer extracted these from the spec: Prerendering cross-origin iframes. Without Supports-Loading-Mode: credentialed-prerender / prerender-cross-origin-frames opt-in header, cross-origin iframes are deferred during prerender. Scripting mode "blocked-until-activation" pauses scripts. document.prerendering true during prerender; prerenderingchange event on activation. UA may destroy prerendering traversable on iframe issues. Authors mus...

openQuestions.title: Reconcile blurb and page contents
openQuestions.detail: The feature index blurb promises behaviours that the page doesn't yet exercise. Either trim the blurb to match what the page does, or add the missing UI.

summary: Workable concept with a couple of gaps. 2 open question(s) queued.
